purpose: Define provider-agnostic per-user mailbox configuration so any authorized provider can be connected later without locking CXOrbia to Gmail, Outlook or a single tenant-level account.
principle: Email must be provider-agnostic, per user and real when authorized. A user may use a corporate email, assigned TyA email, CXOrbia-created email, personal email or manual/fictitious placeholder depending on tenant policy.
configurationRules: Email provider must be selectable per user, not fixed globally.
Tenant may define allowed provider policies, but individual user mailbox remains the operational configuration unit.
A fictitious placeholder email must not send or receive real mail.
Manual contact-only email may be used for traceability before real connection.
Any provider integration requires explicit future authorization and provider-specific secure setup.
Email actions must link to traceable entities when operationally relevant.
securityRules: Do not store provider secrets in repo.
Do not store OAuth tokens in plain text.
Do not enable reading/sending until provider integration and permissions are approved.
Do not expose personal mailbox content to other users unless permission/role allows it.
Do not treat placeholder emails as deliverable addresses.

newLessons: How to create a user with or without mailbox
Difference between personal email, assigned email and placeholder email
When email can read/send and when it is only traceability

hardStops: Do not connect a real provider from this contract.
Do not read real email from this contract.
Do not send real email from this contract.
Do not store secrets or tokens in repo.
Do not treat placeholder email as deliverable.
